seoTitle: Number One vs Numbugga — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Number One and Numbugga. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Number One vs Numbugga: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Number One and Numbugga.
heading: Number One vs Numbugga — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Number One, New South Wales and Numbugga, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Numbugga has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Number One (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Number One or Numbugga safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Number One has a flood risk score of 33/100, while Numbugga scores 37/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Number One scores 39/100 for bushfire risk. Numbugga scores 40/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Number One or Numbugga?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Numbugga has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
